human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for media-distribution boundary discipline, title-version and package evidence handling, caption and localization dependency handling, vendor-risk and fulfillment specificity, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to title-version grounding, asset-order and content-delivery-requirement validation, media package and localization readiness, fulfillment-status handling, completion evidence capture, and bounded escalation tied to media-distribution records.

Authority sources cover AMWA AS-11 and AS-11 X9 delivery guidance, MovieLabs MDDF, Asset Ordering and Delivery, Avails, Media Delivery Core, QC vocabulary, EIDR version-identification rules, SMPTE IMF and timed-text guidance, and FCC captioning obligations.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported closeout, rights-sensitive escalation, technical conformance and QC routing, accessibility hard stops, vendor-risk escalation, terminology fidelity, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/broadcast-film-and-streaming-production-services/distribution-operations-media-specialist/ so distribution operations stays separate from rights approval, media engineering, QC signoff, accessibility waivers, scheduling authority, and legal interpretation.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around rights and avails, engineering, QC, accessibility, partner delivery conflicts, and unsupported closeout.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated distribution operations media specialist that supports evidence-backed media fulfillment and escalation for broadcast, film, and streaming production environments.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
